title: Trump Attacks Pope Leo as 'Weak' and 'Terrible for Foreign Policy' After Pontiff's Iran Peace Appeal
detail: President Trump publicly attacked newly elected Pope Leo XIV, calling him 'weak' and 'terrible for foreign policy' after the pontiff publicly appealed for peace in the Iran conflict. The Pope had urged all parties to pursue a diplomatic resolution to the ongoing US-Iran war. Trump's attack on the head of the Catholic Church sparked immediate diplomatic backlash from European allies and Catholic-majority nations, adding a significant religious dimension to the already-fraught US-Iran crisis.

media.caption: Trump attacks Pope Leo XIV after the pontiff's public appeal for peace in the Iran conflict

title: Trump Acknowledges Gas Prices May Not Fall Before Midterm Elections Amid Hormuz Blockade Energy Shock
detail: President Trump acknowledged publicly that gasoline prices may not fall before the November 2026 midterm elections, underlining significant Republican political vulnerability amid the ongoing Strait of Hormuz blockade and the resulting energy price shock. The admission is a notable political concession, as fuel prices have surged sharply since the Iran conflict began and the Hormuz blockade further disrupted global oil supply chains. Analysts warn high energy costs could be a defining issue for Republican candidates in competitive districts.

media.caption: Trump acknowledges gas prices may not fall before midterms amid Hormuz blockade energy shock
